model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Duke holds a commanding 5.3-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Florida (+39.17 vs +33.83), driven by the nation's best defense (AdjD 88.7) that is roughly 3 points per 100 possessions better than Florida's already excellent 5th-ranked unit (AdjD 91.8), while also boasting a superior offense (AdjO 127.9 vs 125.6). The loss of starting point guard Caleb Foster to foot surgery and Isaiah Evans to an ACC Tournament injury significantly narrows this gap, but Duke has adapted throughout the tournament with Cayden Boozer stepping into an expanded role and Cooper Flagg performing at a generational level, and the Blue Devils already beat Florida 67-66 in December's ACC/SEC Challenge. Florida's championship pedigree as defending national champions and their battle-tested tournament path give them a very credible upset path, but Duke's two-way statistical superiority across every dimension makes the Blue Devils the narrow favorite in this 1-vs-1 seed Final Four clash.

reasoningStep.title: National Semifinal 1: Duke vs Florida
reasoningStep.summary: Duke's historically elite defense and overall statistical superiority give them the edge despite significant personnel losses, though Florida's championship experience and elite two-way profile make this a competitive Final Four matchup.
evidence: Duke ranks #1 nationally in both KenPom (AdjEM +39.17) and Torvik with the nation's best defense (AdjD 88.7) and 4th-ranked offense (AdjO 127.9)
Florida ranks #4 in KenPom with AdjEM +33.83, AdjD 91.8 (5th), and AdjO 125.6 (9th)
Duke beat Florida 67-66 in December's ACC/SEC Challenge on a late Isaiah Evans 3-pointer
Duke is missing Caleb Foster (foot fracture surgery) and Isaiah Evans (ACC Tournament injury), but Cayden Boozer has stepped into expanded role
Florida is the defending national champion with elite championship experience under Todd Golden
Florida's Alex Condon dealt with ankle injury in Sweet 16 but gave optimistic update
The ~5.3-point efficiency margin gap is substantial even accounting for Duke's personnel losses
Both teams are 1-seeds, making this one of the marquee Final Four matchups between elite programs

result.text: Gators Alex Condon Gives Optimistic Update on Ankle Injury Skip to main content SI.COM ON SI SI SWIMSUIT SI TICKETS SI RESORTS SI SHOPS MY ACCOUNT SUBSCRIBE NOW FOOTBALL RECRUITING BASKETBALL BASEBALL NEWSLETTER SI.COM SI.COM GATORS FB SI.COM GATORS BB FOOTBALL RECRUITING BASKETBALL BASEBALL FOOTBALL FOOTBALL NEWS SCHEDULE STATS RANKINGS SCORES BASKETBALL BASKETBALL NEWS SCHEDULE STATS RANKINGS SCORES More from Florida Gators On SI RECRUITING BASEBALL NEWSLETTER SI.COM SI.COM GATORS FB SI.COM GATORS BB Gators Alex Condon Gives Optimistic Update on Ankle Injury He's been cautious with his ankle, but the Florida Gators' big man is feeling good about his chances of playing in the Elite Eight against Texas Tech Harrison Smajovits | Share on Facebook Share on X Add us as a preferred source Condon is playing it safe to ensure he can play | Bob Donnan-Imagn Images In this story: Florida Gators The Florida Gators are set to take on Texas Tech in the Elite Eight round of the NCAA Tournament. A major question mark is the status of big man Alex Condon. --> The Florida Gators are set to take on Texas Tech in the Elite Eight round of the NCAA Tournament. A major question mark is the status of big man Alex Condon. So far, Condon said things are looking promising and he expects to play. --> So far, Condon said things are looking promising and he expects to play. “Feel pretty good, a lot of treatment over the past 12 hours or so,” Condon said Friday. “Getting with the trainers, making sure my ankle is going to be good for the Texas [Tech] game. I feel like I'll be ready to go for tomorrow.” --> “Feel pretty good, a lot of treatment over the past 12 hours or so,” Condon said Friday. “Getting with the trainers, making sure my ankle is going to be good for the Texas [Tech] game. I feel like I'll be ready to go for tomorrow.” Condon added that he didn't practice Friday out of precaution. The strategy is to keep weight off his foot so he can rest ahead of the game. --> Condon added that he didn't practice Friday out of precaution. The strategy is to keep weight off his foot so he can rest ahead of the game. In the win over Maryland in the Sweet 16, Condon suffered an ankle injury that took him out for part of the game. Condon could be seen just off the court getting some work in on a stationary bike. He exited the game with 12:16 left in the first half and wouldn't return until there was 13:29 left in the second half. He finished with just six points. --> In the win over Maryland in the Sweet 16, Condon suffered an ankle injury that took him out for part of the game. Condon could be seen just off the court getting some work in on a stationary bike. He exited the game with 12:16 left in the first half and wouldn't return until there was 13:29 left in the second half. He finished with just six points. “Initially, it was just a bit of a shock. Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me synthesize everything for this Final Four matchup.

**Key findings:**

**Duke (1-seed East, ACC, 31-2):**
- KenPom #1: AdjO 127.9 (4th), AdjD 88.7 (best in nation), AdjEM +39.17
- Torvik #1: AdjOE 128.0 (4th), AdjDE 90.4 (1st)
- Missing: Caleb Foster (starting PG, foot fracture/surgery), Isaiah Evans (injured ACC Tournament)
- Cooper Flagg healthy and dominant; Cayden Boozer stepped up
- Beat Florida 67-66 in December (Evans hit go-ahead 3)

**Florida (1-seed South, SEC, 26-7):**
- KenPom #4: AdjO 125.6 (9th), AdjD 91.8 (top 5), AdjEM +33.83
- Torvik #4: AdjOE 126.1 (11th), AdjDE 92.2 (5th)
- Defending national champions
- Alex Condon had ankle injury in Sweet 16 but gave optimistic update for Elite Eight
- Rueben Chinyelu anchors elite defense
- Lost to Vanderbilt in SEC Tournament semifinal
- Beat Houston in Elite Eight (prior pick, narrow 0.54 confidence)

**Analysis:**
- Duke has a significant ~5.3-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage (+39.17 vs +33.83)
- Duke's defense (AdjD 88.7) is meaningfully better than Florida's (91.8) — about 3 points per 100 possessions
- Duke's offense (AdjO 127.9) is also better than Florida's (125.6) — about 2.3 points per 100 possessions
- Duke won the head-to-head in December 67-66, though that was with Foster and Evans healthy
- Duke is now missing Foster (starting PG) and Evans (key shooter who hit the game-winner vs Florida)
- Florida has championship experience and pedigree as defending champs
- Florida may have Condon at less than 100% with ankle issues
- Florida's path was tougher (beat Houston in Elite Eight), potentially more battle-tested but also more fatigued

The personnel losses for Duke (Foster, Evans) are significant but the Blue Devils have adapted throughout the tournament. The ~5-point efficiency margin gap is substantial even accounting for those losses. Duke's defense is historically elite and should challenge Florida's offense.
